description: Where trees and surrounding life substantially shape a place, the place is a 'shelira'. Forests may be open or dense, young or old, tended or growing with little direct care. 'koshira' (grove) is the smaller stand; legal and ecological documents can draw a sharper boundary for their own purpose.
articulatory_notes: A narrow stream of unvoiced air passes the raised tongue blade for 'sh' and opens into front 'e'. Stressed 'li' moves contact to the alveolar ridge; a tap or trill releases that contact into final 'a'.
usage_notes: This is a place and living community, not one organism, so a living classifier would misdescribe it. Tree and animal nouns can come first to identify a particular kind of forest.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A forest is more than standing timber. Water and soil shape it, other lives inhabit it, and human care or memory may already belong there.

semantic_domains.nature: a tree-shaped habitat and living community
semantic_domains.spatial: an area understood through its wooded character
